title: erica manipuliflora subsp. anthura
parts: Erica manipuliflora subsp. anthura has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and stems, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Erica manipuliflora subsp. anthura contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of the plant are rich in phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems. The roots of Erica manipuliflora subsp. anthura contain saponins and phenolic acids, which are used to treat digestive issues and other health conditions.
side_effects: Erica manipuliflora subsp. anthura can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain due to the presence of toxic compounds like grayanotoxins. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extracts can cause skin redness, itching, and rashes, particularly in individuals with sensitive skin. Additionally, using Erica manipuliflora subsp. anthura as a medicinal herb without proper knowledge and precautions can interact with other medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
